kwargs.title: Set navigation to Andorra la Vella with charging stop and nearby open fast food in the 19:00-19:45 window

Confirm the navigation result to the user and note any unresolved limitations or approximations

Main route to Andorra la Vella retrieved. Fastest and shortest coincide on route rll_mon_and_156882 with 596.53 km and 7 h 19 min, no toll.
Converted requested 19:00-19:45 stop window into approximately 149.5 km to 210.6 km along fastest route rll_mon_and_156882.

Within the requested window along the fastest route, found reachable charging station EDF Pulse at about 154.11 km / 1 h 56 min arrival, 24/7, with available 350 kW DC plug. Nearby fast food found around same route position is Brioche Dorée but it closes at 18:00. Open fast food found later in the window is McDonald's near 200 km, open until 23:00, but no available charging station was found nearby in that route area. No charging-station stop with open fast food in the requested 19:00-19:45 window could be identified.
Navigation not set because no stop satisfied all user constraints; waiting for user decision on fallback or changed constraints.
Need to communicate limitation and offer closest alternatives.

kwargs.content:
I checked it for you, and I can’t fully set that up as requested.

Right now there isn’t a matching stop that satisfies all of your constraints at once: a charging station stop between 19:00 and 19:45, with fast food nearby that’s open, and with the stop itself being the charger.

What I found is this. The main route to Andorra la Vella is about 596.5 kilometers and 7 hours 19 minutes. The fastest and shortest route are the same, and it does not include tolls. Since your navigation isn’t active yet, I’m not starting guidance without your go-ahead.

For the stop window, 19:00 to 19:45 translates to roughly 149.5 to 210.6 kilometers into the drive.

Best charging match:
EDF Pulse, about 154.1 kilometers from here, around 19:06 arrival, open 24 hours, with an available 350 kilowatt DC plug.

Food near that charger:
Brioche Dorée is nearby, but it closes at 18:00, so it won’t be open in your requested window.

Open fast food in the window:
There’s a McDonald’s around the 200 kilometer mark, open until 23:00, but I did not find an available charging station in that same area.

So I’ve got two close fallback options if you want:
Option one, use EDF Pulse as the charging stop around 19:06 and give up the open fast food requirement.
Option two, keep the open fast food requirement and relax the charging stop timing or location constraint.

If you want, I can also just present the route to Andorra la Vella in the normal way and you can tell me whether to start navigation.

Yep, there is.

Near that McDonald’s at the 200 kilometer mark, I found Engie Electric. It’s open 24 hours, about 4.2 kilometers off the route with roughly a 6 minute detour, and it has a 300 kilowatt DC plug. Right now that plug is occupied, so I can’t promise availability when you get there, but the station itself is there.

So if fast food is the priority, this is the best match I’ve found:
McDonald’s at around 200 kilometers, open until 23:00
Engie Electric nearby at the same route area
Charging stop would still be the station, not the restaurant

And yep, for the drive to Andorra la Vella, the fastest route is also the shortest one here: 596.5 kilometers, 7 hours 19 minutes, no tolls.

If you want, say start navigation on the fastest route with a charging stop at Engie Electric, and I’ll set it up.

Since final navigation is multi-stop and no saved route preference exists, selected fastest route per segment: rlp_mon_cha_848257 Monaco to Engie Electric, and rpl_cha_and_296241 Engie Electric to Andorra la Vella. Navigation set successfully.
Communicate successful navigation setup including chosen stop and note that the charger plug was occupied at lookup time.

then Engie Electric to Andorra la Vella

Tesla Supercharger is also open 24 hours, but right now its DC plugs appear occupied, so only AC seems available there at the moment.

If you want, I can give you directions to Ionity now, or start navigation to Hamburg on your chosen B432 and B132 route after that.

Yes, Ionity. Calculate how long charging will take if I charge to 95%. Then, set up navigation to Hamburg with the charging stop. Take the fastest route to the charging station and the B432, B132 route to Hamburg.

kwargs.content:
I rechecked it, and your navigation is already updated.

Your current route now goes Leipzig to Frankfurt to Barcelona, so Barcelona is set as the final destination.

For the new Frankfurt to Barcelona segment, the active route is the fastest one at 1257.75 kilometers and 15 hours 46 minutes via K105, K121 and L558. No toll roads there.

There are 2 more route options for that segment if you want. I can tell you the alternatives now.

Yep, you’ll definitely need to charge on the way.

Your remaining range is 466 kilometers, and the full trip to Barcelona is about 1594.08 kilometers, so one charge won’t be enough.

You’re short by roughly 1128.08 kilometers compared with the current range, so you should plan multiple charging stops for this trip.

If you want, I can look for charging stations along your current route and help pick good stop points.

Found one good option for you: Fastned.

It matches your charging preference pretty well, since it’s about 50 kilometers into the Frankfurt to Barcelona segment, which is very close to the point where you’d be down to around 15 percent.

It’s open 24 hours, has a 10 minute detour, and currently has available DC plugs. The best available one is a 100 kilowatt DC plug. There’s also a 50 kilowatt DC plug and a 22 kilowatt AC plug available.

Want me to add Fastned as a charging stop in your route?

task.persona: You are 60 years old. Your conversation style when using the voice assistant is 'Commanding: straightforward, imperative sentences. You see the assistant as a executor.'. You are regularly using the voice assistant and understand automotive terminology and technical terms like HVAC, ambient lighting, regenerative braking.
task.instruction: You are on a European road trip driving from Leipzig to Hamburg with a stop in Frankfurt. You currently have active navigation set up for this multi-stop route (Leipzig → Frankfurt → Hamburg). However, you've decided you want to change your final destination from Hamburg to Barcelona instead, as you've gotten excited about visiting Spain. Actively ask the assistant for multiple route options to Barcelona. When presented with multiple route options, choose the shortest route (1257.75km from Frankfurt to Barcelona, no tolls). You want the assistant to set up the new navigation. After setting up the new navigation, you see that you have 98% battery charge, which gives you enough range to comfortably reach Frankfurt, however, you are concerned about your electric vehicle's range for the much longer journey to Barcelona and want to make sure you can reach Barcelona safely. When the assistant tells you a charging station is needed, tell them that he should search for a suitable charging station. It is expected that the assistant disambiguates internally which point along the route is suitable (from a stored preference) for the charging station - do not specify a suitable point yourself and do not tell the assistant about the stored preference. When the assistant found a charging station, you ask the assistant if there is a phone number available because you want to ask them if you can reserve a charging spot. Confirm or request that the assistant should call the phone number.

charging_stations: user always wants to charge around the point where he has 15% state of charge left
